question: Will United States be the furthest advancing host nation at the World Cup?
description:
This market will resolve in favor of the host nation (Canada, Mexico, United States) that advances to the latest stage in the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If there is a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the nation who recorded more total wins through all main tournament rounds of the 2026 FIFA World Cup. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve in favor of the nation who scores more total goals through all main tournament rounds of the 2026 FIFA World Cup. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve in favor of the nation who conceded fewer goals through all main tournament rounds of the 2026 FIFA World Cup. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve to the nation whose listed name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is cancelled, postponed after August 2, 2026, 11:59 PM ET or it cannot be determined which nation advanced the furthest during the 2026 FIFA World Cup within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from FIFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: World Cup: Furthest Advancing Host Nation

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup group stage results have kept the furthest-advancing host nation market tight, with the United States, Mexico, and Canada all positioned as plausible contenders based on early results and draw context. The U.S. and Mexico opened with convincing wins that boosted implied probabilities to the mid-50s and mid-30s respectively on Polymarket, while Canada's draw left it as a longer shot. Pre-tournament FIFA rankings placed the U.S. and Mexico near each other in the global top 20 and Canada lower, but all three received favorable group placements as top seeds in an expanded 48-team field. This setup, combined with similar overall squad strength and the potential for any host to reach the knockout rounds or beyond, sustains competitive dynamics among the leaders rather than allowing one to pull away decisively.
